## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On September 24, 1932, the incredibly massive, highly historic, and fiercely pivotal Poona Pact was officially and aggressively signed at the Yerwada Central Jail. Ambedkar and high-caste Hindu leaders fundamentally abandoned separate electorates for Dalits. In absolute exchange, it aggressively guaranteed incredibly massive, reserved electoral seats for the "Depressed Classes," heavily forging the foundational, massive political architecture of Indian caste reservations.

On September 24, 1861, Bhikaiji Cama, the incredibly massive, fiercely courageous, and absolute foundational heroine of the militant Indian independence movement, was officially born in Bombay (Mumbai).
